programmes.show_description: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz where six hopefuls compete for £1 million. However, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. The discovery of a new variant of Covid-19 causes chaos in Heathrow as airports around the world begin to close their borders to the UK. As flights are cancelled before the passengers' eyes, they scramble to get on any available plane in a desperate attempt to get home

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by broadcaster Kaye Adams, who enjoys nasi goreng for breakfast and smacked cucumber and dumpling salad. Phillippa Gunn gives a lesson on creating easy colourful landscape paintings, Shivi learns how to navigate using nature with bushcraft and survival expert Mark Beckham and Jimmy gets up close and personal with the wildlife park's foxes
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

The competition is heating up with just 41 players left as Rob Brydon hosts another tense round of quick-fire quiz duels, with each win expanding a player's territory, while each loss sends another home. With a £5,000 Floor bonus on the line and a £50,000 jackpot up for grabs at the end of the series, it's all to play for as friends turn to foes and challenge one another
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more
The key headlines from the day so far
Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with Ade Adepitan, Clare Balding and Robert Webb guessing phrases for the chance to win £50,000 for charity

Game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot. Crossing words to beat the reigning champions this time are sisters Lauren and Ellie from Solihull and friends Gill and Sue
Stephen Mulhern hosts another episode of the exciting and iconic gameshow as a brave contestant takes on the infamous Banker for a chance at winning a life-changing cash prize.

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh hosts the show where thousands of pounds are at stake. All that stands between Mary from London, Robyn from Plymouth, Giles from Cardiff, Torran originally from Manchester and that money is the small matter of one of the country's finest quiz brains

programmes.show_name: STV News at Six and Weather
programmes.show_description: Including regional weather forecast
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

England v Uruguay (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the friendly at Wembley Stadium, as both teams continue their preparations for this summer's FIFA World Cup finals. The last time these teams met was in the group stage of the 2014 World Cup, when a brace by Luis Suarez gave the South Americans a 2-1 win. Suarez has recently retired from international football, but the Uruguayans are still likely to provide a stern test for Thomas Tuchel's men. With analysis from Roy Keane and Ian Wright, and commentary by Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programmes.show_name: Devil in Disguise: John Wayne Gacy
programmes.show_description: The life of Jeffrey Rignall, one of Gacy's few surviving victims, is chronicled. The killer's execution day causes massive protests and reunions as victims' family members and those who worked the case seek closure, and Amirante tries to move on.

Jeremy Clarkson hosts the exciting and fast-paced quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for £1 million - however, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

Documentary about the small North Yorkshire town of Bentham, which two years ago was recorded as the most contaminated place in the UK for PFAS - toxic so-called 'forever chemicals', some of which are linked to cancers and other serious illnesses. The film reveals the likely source of the pollution - a local factory that until 2025 manufactured firefighting foam - and follows residents and former workers, some battling cancer, as they undergo blood tests to find out whether the pollution in the environment has entered their bodies
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Ant and Dec welcome firefighters Harry and Ben, who are confident that they can handle the pressure of the Limitless Ladder to win a life-changing amount of money. Ben dreams of taking his family to Disneyland, while Harry is determined to buy a new house with his partner. It is then the turn of husband and wife Ciaran and Louise, who have set their sights on a six-figure win, and also on buying a racehorse
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as former EastEnders star Natalie Cassidy and TV host Angellica Bell take on athletes Jonnie Peacock and Greg Rutherford for charity

Alan Titchmarsh and team are in Salford to show how to create an eco-inspired kitchen garden. Motivated by a community hero who likes to feed everyone and is passionate about recycling, Alan, Frances Tophill and Katie Rushworth recycle, re-use and go green to create a quaint, mix and match garden that's designed to provide a bounty of flowers and food, right down to converting an old bath into a pond for watercress

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Alan is joined at Manor Farm by actress Sarah Parish and singer Peter Andre. He also catches up with the zebras currently residing in Warwickshire, while a couple of llamas make quite an impression. Plus, advice on reviving houseplants after the winter months
A round-up of the latest headlines
James is joined by double Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, chefs Meliz Berg and John Hooker, and First Dates barman Merlin Griffiths

The chef heads to Castle of Mey in Caithness, once home to Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother and now a treasured place to King Charles. Head gardener Chris tells Raymond about the history of the walled garden and reveals how they grow produce in such a challenging location, while chef Martin cooks an Aberdeen Angus fillet steak with seasonal vegetables from the gardens

James goes on a culinary road trip around the UK and kicks off his travels in the Orkneys, where he is joined by fellow chef Nick Nairn. He shops for hand-dived scallops, before setting up his mobile kitchen in Stromness harbour to make barbecue scallops with black pudding, apple and hazelnut butter. He also meets the owner of a unique herd of sheep in the remote island of North Ronaldsay
Round-up of the latest headlines

Francesca Cumani presents coverage of a meeting headlined by the Lincoln Handicap from Doncaster, plus racing from Kempton Park. Analysis comes from Adele Mulrennan and Jason Weaver, with reports by Oli Bell and commentary from Richard Hoiles

Jeremy Clarkson hosts a quiz in which six hopefuls compete for £1 million, with only one player at a time able to take a place in the Hot Seat and answer multiple-choice questions to work the way up the Money Ladder. Each question is against the clock and if a player gets one wrong they are eliminated, the Top Prize drops down, and the next player takes their place

Bradley Walsh presents as Ortis Deley, Maggie Aderin, Graeme Hall and Sarah Millican pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
Round-up of the latest headlines

Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Samia Longchambon, Amy Dowden and Will Best all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

Ant and Dec host as the auditions return to Blackpool's Winter Gardens, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. With the presenters yet to push their Golden Buzzer, might one of today's performers be just what the pair are looking for?

Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in a fake love-themed show hosted by Matt and Emma Willis, who are also in on the whole thing and even get set their own mission in a hot tub. Plus, Olivia Attwood is the guest saboteur

Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

Jonathan is joined by Celebrity Traitors star and ex-England rugby union player Joe Marler, comedian Romesh Ranganathan and actress and stand-up Aisling Bea, while music comes from UK-based American soul musician Jalen Ngonda
